deep_dive.title: WaspMOT: A Benchmark for Long-Term Multi-Object Tracking of Trichogramma Wasps
deep_dive.abstract: Multi-object tracking (MOT) has achieved strong performance on benchmarks dominated by short video sequences. However, such datasets do not adequately evaluate long-term identity preservation, where objects must be tracked consistently over extended durations. We introduce WaspMOT, a benchmark designed to address this gap through long-duration tracking of Trichogramma wasps in controlled ecological experiments. The dataset contains 10 sequences of approximately 12,000 frames each (over 8 minutes at 25 FPS), with dense MOTChallenge annotations and oracle detections to isolate association performance.   Unlike existing benchmarks, WaspMOT forms a closed-set tracking scenario where all individuals remain present throughout the sequence, requiring consistent identity assignment across thousands of frames despite abrupt jumps, occlusions, and highly similar appearance. We establish a benchmark by evaluating five tracking-by-detection methods, including ByteTrack, BoT-SORT, C-BIoU, OC-SORT, and McByte, under a unified protocol. Results show that all methods suffer from significant trajectory fragmentation, highlighting the difficulty of long-term identity preservation even with perfect detections. A simple spatial tracklet stitching baseline consistently improves performance, indicating that substantial gains remain possible.   WaspMOT provides a new benchmark for studying long-term association and reveals limitations of current tracking approaches that are not observable on conventional datasets. The benchmark will be made publicly available at the project repository: https://github.com/tstanczyk95/WaspMOT/ .
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: multi-object tracking benchmark for long-term identity preservation
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 多目标跟踪基准，针对长期身份保持
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 多目标跟踪基准，针对长期身份保持

deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Introduces WaspMOT, a benchmark for long-term multi-object tracking with dense annotations, challenging identity persistence.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对现有MOT基准过于关注短序列而忽视长期身份保持的问题，提出了WaspMOT基准。该基准包含长持续时间（每段约8000帧）的胡蜂多目标跟踪视频，提供密集的MOTChallenge风格标注和Oracle检测。WaspMOT形成一个闭合场景，所有个体始终存在，侧重于评估长期关联性能，为视觉目标跟踪领域提供了极具挑战性的长期跟踪基准。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该论文针对现有MOT基准过于关注短序列而忽视长期身份保持的问题，提出了WaspMOT基准。该基准包含长持续时间（每段约8000帧）的胡蜂多目标跟踪视频，提供密集的MOTChallenge风格标注和Oracle检测。WaspMOT形成一个闭合场景，所有个体始终存在，侧重于评估长期关联性能，为视觉目标跟踪领域提供了极具挑战性的长期跟踪基准。

deep_dive.title: REMIND: RE-Identification with Memory for INDoor Navigation
deep_dive.abstract: Mobile robots operating indoors must re-identify previously observed objects after long temporal gaps, significant viewpoint changes, and severe illumination variations. This remains a challenging problem: multi-object tracking methods are optimized for short-term association of pedestrians and vehicles at video rates, person and vehicle re-identification approaches lack persistent memory mechanisms, and state-of-the-art video object segmentation techniques rely on reactive distractor filtering rather than enforcing global identity consistency.   To address these limitations, we present REMIND, an online tracker designed for long-term multi-object re-identification of generic indoor objects from monocular RGB imagery, requiring neither camera pose nor depth. Motivated by evidence from visual cognition that humans rely on accumulated appearance familiarity and spatial context rather than explicit self-localization, REMIND combines frozen DINOv3 features with a dual-bank multi-prototype appearance memory, part- and background-level descriptors, a neighbour-context reasoning module exploiting spatial co-occurrence, and joint Hungarian assignment with ambiguity-aware safeguards. On a purpose-built indoor dataset featuring controlled revisits and dense same-class clutter, REMIND reaches 90.35% IDF1, nearly 20 points above a state-of-the-art video object segmentation baseline and more than 36 above a strong tracking-by-detection baseline. On ScanNet++, it attains the highest IDF1 in every setting but one, end-to-end detection over all scenes, where the tracking-by-detection baseline is marginally ahead while REMIND still associates and recovers identities more accurately; it also completes every scene, whereas the video object segmentation baseline exhausts GPU memory on 66.9% under YOLO detections. The complete system, evaluation framework, and dataset are publicly released.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Long-term multi-object re-identification tracker for indoor objects; relevant to visual object tracking
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 用于室内物体的长期多目标重识别跟踪器，与视觉目标跟踪相关
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 用于室内物体的长期多目标重识别跟踪器，与视觉目标跟踪相关
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 用于室内物体的长期多目标重识别跟踪器，与视觉目标跟踪相关
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: REMIND is an online tracker for long-term multi-object re-identification of generic indoor objects from monocular RGB, using persistent memory to ensure identity consistency over long gaps.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: REMIND针对室内机器人长期多目标重识别问题，提出在线跟踪器，仅需单目RGB图像，无需相机位姿或深度。通过持久记忆机制解决长时间间隔、视角变化和光照变化下的身份一致性。实验在合成和真实数据集上展示了优于现有重识别和跟踪方法的表现，推动了移动机器人在室内场景中的鲁棒目标跟踪。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: REMIND针对室内机器人长期多目标重识别问题，提出在线跟踪器，仅需单目RGB图像，无需相机位姿或深度。通过持久记忆机制解决长时间间隔、视角变化和光照变化下的身份一致性。实验在合成和真实数据集上展示了优于现有重识别和跟踪方法的表现，推动了移动机器人在室内场景中的鲁棒目标跟踪。

quick_skim.title: Equivariant Filter for High Performance Image Tracking using an Event Camera
quick_skim.abstract: Image tracking is the problem of estimating the transformation that relates a moving image of a scene to an original reference image. The problem is important in control of autonomous vehicles or robots, where the image encodes information about the motion of the camera or environment, as well as in pure computer vision applications. In this paper, we present an equivariant filter design for high performance tracking of planar image transformations using an event camera. The design exploits the Asynchronous Event Blob (AEB) tracker (Wang et al., 2024) to extract feature-position measurements from the raw event stream, and an equivariant filter to compute an affine image translation and rotation using the special Euclidean group symmetry. The equivariant filter incorporates an equivalent-measurement update step that de-correlates the (highly temporally correlated) feature-position measurements provided by the AEB tracker. We evaluate the design experimentally using two datasets involving general and fast rotational motion. We benchmark results against direct optimisation (estimating the relative transformation from the raw blob tracks), and a covariance intersection approach for overcoming data correlation. Our design provides smooth image tracking for features moving up to 7000 pixels per second on the image plane.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Image tracking with event camera; relevant to visual tracking
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 使用事件相机的图像跟踪，与视觉目标跟踪相关
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 使用事件相机的图像跟踪，与视觉目标跟踪相关
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 使用事件相机的图像跟踪，与视觉目标跟踪相关
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: An equivariant filter for high-performance tracking of planar image transformations using an event camera, exploiting group symmetry for robust estimation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对事件相机的高性能图像跟踪问题，提出了一种等变滤波器设计，利用特殊欧几里得群对称性计算仿射图像变换。该方法结合异步事件块跟踪器提取特征点，在激烈运动场景下实现鲁棒的跟踪。实验表明，该方法在跟踪平面图像变换上具有高精度和实时性，为视觉跟踪领域提供了事件相机方案。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对事件相机的高性能图像跟踪问题，提出了一种等变滤波器设计，利用特殊欧几里得群对称性计算仿射图像变换。该方法结合异步事件块跟踪器提取特征点，在激烈运动场景下实现鲁棒的跟踪。实验表明，该方法在跟踪平面图像变换上具有高精度和实时性，为视觉跟踪领域提供了事件相机方案。

quick_skim.title: PreSIST: Vision-Language-Informed Object Persistence Prediction in Open-World Scenes
quick_skim.abstract: Robots deployed over long periods must reason about environments that change over time. Existing long-term perception systems often address object change reactively, updating their maps only after revisiting a scene and observing that an object has moved. Instead, robots should reason proactively about how long objects are likely to persist using the context in which they appear. For example, a car at a traffic light and a car in a parking spot share the same semantic class, but their contexts imply different persistence durations. We propose PreSIST (Predictive Scene-conditioned Instance Survival over Time), a method for predicting whether an observed object will remain in its last seen pose at arbitrary future times. PreSIST estimates instance-level persistence priors from object properties and scene context, then integrates these priors with a probabilistic persistence filter as observations become available. Its key insight is that the reasoning capabilities of vision-language models (VLMs) can relate scene context to likely object use and human activity, enabling persistence prediction before long-term observations are available. We develop two interchangeable variants: PreSIST-Lang, which estimates persistence priors using a VLM, and PreSIST-Vis, a novel vision-only model trained using PreSIST-Lang pseudo-labels for efficient deployment. Experiments on a new dataset of in-the-wild object persistence annotations show that PreSIST-Lang and PreSIST-Vis outperform baselines on open-world persistence prediction.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 视觉语言信息驱动的物体持久性预测
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 视觉语言信息驱动的物体持久性预测
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 视觉语言信息驱动的物体持久性预测
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: PreSIST predicts object persistence in open-world scenes using vision-language context, enabling proactive long-term perception for robots.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对长期部署机器人需主动推理环境变化的问题，提出PreSIST方法，利用视觉语言上下文预测物体在场景中的持续时长。模型整合场景上下文，区分不同语义类别的持久性差异。实验证明该方法能有效预测物体未来位置，为视觉语言跟踪提供先验知识。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文针对长期部署机器人需主动推理环境变化的问题，提出PreSIST方法，利用视觉语言上下文预测物体在场景中的持续时长。模型整合场景上下文，区分不同语义类别的持久性差异。实验证明该方法能有效预测物体未来位置，为视觉语言跟踪提供先验知识。
